questions.question: Explain the main limitations of the Boolean retrieval model.
expectedPoints: No ranking of results - all matching documents are equally relevant
No partial matching - documents either match completely or not at all
Requires users to understand Boolean logic
Cannot handle term weighting or frequency information

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Vector Space Model and Probabilistic Model approaches to information retrieval.
expectedPoints: VSM represents documents and queries as vectors in term space, while probabilistic model uses probability distributions
VSM uses cosine similarity for ranking, probabilistic model uses probability of relevance
VSM handles partial matching naturally, probabilistic model requires relevance assumptions
Probabilistic model has stronger theoretical foundation (Probability Ranking Principle)
VSM is more intuitive geometrically, probabilistic model handles document length better
Both models require parameter tuning and have evolved into modern variants
